messages.content: Can you show me the proper SQL for MySQL to copy any rows from a table with a pkey under a certain value and another attribute equal to a certain value? There are three tables. The parent table A, child of parent table A table B with a foreign key referencing A, and child of table B table C linked to B through a link table B_C. What SQL would properly copy a record from A and all it's children? Modify the SQL to copy the records into the same tables they came from. Then wrap the SQL into a revertible block.

SELECT * FROM A WHERE A.pkey < {some value}

FOR EACH ROW IN result
    INSERT INTO A_SOURCE.TABLE_A (columns)
    VALUES (ROW.*)

    INSERT INTO TABLE_B (columns)
    VALUES (ROW.*)

    INSERT INTO TABLE_C (columns)
    VALUES (ROW.*)

    DELETE ROW WHERE ROW.A.pkey = {some value}

You now have a copy of A with all it's children inserted into the same tables they came from. You can now revert the change removing the rows from A and it's children that were inserted.

SELECT * FROM A WHERE A.pkey < {some value}

FOR EACH ROW IN result
    INSERT INTO A_SOURCE.TABLE_A (columns)
    VALUES (ROW.*)

    INSERT INTO TABLE_B (columns)
    VALUES (ROW.*)

    INSERT INTO TABLE_C (columns)
    VALUES (ROW.*)

    DELETE ROW WHERE ROW.A.pkey = {some value}

Wipe table A and it's children tables B and C
